name: switch
title: Switch
description: An immediate on/off setting built on Base UI's Switch. Exposes role=switch with aria-checked, Space toggles, and a required visible label that doubles as part of the 44px pointer target. State is signaled by thumb POSITION (not color alone), so it survives forced-colors mode. Optional linked description, disabled state, controlled and uncontrolled, and Field wiring.
useWhen: Toggling a single setting that takes effect immediately (notifications on/off, dark mode)
A binary preference with no separate Save step
Replacing a lone checkbox where the choice is an instant setting rather than a form value
avoidWhen: A selection or consent that is submitted with a form (use a Checkbox)
Choosing exactly one option from a set (use a radio-group)
Selecting multiple options from a list (use checkboxes)
Triggering an immediate action rather than a persistent state (use a button)

keyboard: Tab moves focus to the switch; Shift+Tab moves away
Space toggles between on and off
A disabled switch is removed from the tab order and cannot toggle
Clicking the label text also toggles the switch (the label is part of the target)
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (the padded label row is the pointer target)
notes: The control exposes role=switch and aria-checked; the label is required and provides the accessible name via aria-labelledby.

Non-color redundancy: the thumb POSITION (moved to the inline-end when on) signals state, so on/off remains distinguishable in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode where the checked fill color is overridden.
The track and thumb keep visible borders so forced-colors mode paints a boundary; the thumb slide is gated behind motion-safe.
An optional description is linked with aria-describedby and excluded from the accessible name.
